messages.text: My view and it’s just an opinion is to let’s see how it goes. I think the benefit of Telegram is that it allows for a better flow of conversation. Like any conversation if people are talking about one topic and then someone drops a random off-topic comment then it generally doesn’t get any traction and just breaks the flow. Seems quiet at the moment, good time as any to start a conversation. People can join in or not. For the record I think they’ll stay up 🤝

messages.text: TBH. This Group was set up for those like me who just don’t need another platform and because the old adage “if it ain’t broke don’t fix it” came to mind. I’m guessing and I’ve no skin in the game, flipping another (potential) hundred or so YT subscribers worked better from an 👀 perspective than the £5 per month from a couple of hundred Telegram subscribers. As I say, I’ve no skin in the game and if Blue Monday wanted to return to Telegram I’m sure we’d all welcome that as well. No plans to branch out beyond this, not in it for 💰 just a resting home for those who want to chat Footy, ITFC in a grown up space, self-moderated by the group. Hope it works for people.

messages.text:
Damning report on Scotland strike force prospects for the world cup. (George Hirst interest):

"There was further cause to feel worried about Scotland's lack of edge up front. George Hirst worked himself to a standstill, created a few half-chances for himself, but couldn't execute.

In the Lyndon Dykes role, Hirst did a better version of Lyndon Dykes than Lyndon Dykes. He was mobile, powerful, full of running.

But these two suffer from the same ailment - they're strikers who are not very good at striking the ball into an opponent's net no matter who they're playing for." (BBC Sport)😦

text_entities.text:
Damning report on Scotland strike force prospects for the world cup. (George Hirst interest):

In the Lyndon Dykes role, Hirst did a better version of Lyndon Dykes than Lyndon Dykes. He was mobile, powerful, full of running.

text:
We are a new group formed to support our Chairman, Mark Ashton, and our Prime Minister-in-waiting Nigel Farage (and Reform UK). Despite the screaming from the far-left members of groups such as Blue Action, TWTD, and this Ipswich “Collective,” the move has proven to be overwhelmingly popular with regular Town fans like us, ordinary working-class people fed up with the political establishment and desperately wanting a change of Government. 

We commend Mark Ashton's initiative to meet our future Prime Minister and lobby him in the best interests of our great club (and national game). He shouldn’t have had to apologise for doing the right thing, and the national and international media condemnation painting Farage as a racist and far right is yet more proof of how low the political establishment will sink to try and discredit our Prime Minister and Reform UK.
We are aware that the members of the far left are planning a protest to disrupt the game on Monday, arranged in part over at TWTD, and we’ll be making an announcement on this in due course.

messages.text:
The move does seem to have created a split, half the group moving on and half staying here. I’ve joined both.

This has the more anarchic and lively chat, which I enjoy, but I felt I should support BM as independent ITFC media and there is a decent amount of effort in creating the content. More of a thank you than paying for Discord access, as I’m not really using it. Early days, but I can’t imagine splitting a community in two is going to be beneficial.

messages.text:
Maybe, although I don’t want to celebrate that as for some in this chat it’s a lot of fivers lost and they’re trying to grow a good business. I like that fact that this group has been kept together though 👍 .

The Discord will continue to grow, those who don’t like the look and feel of the new app will soon be replaced by those accustomed to Discord or new participants where it’s all they’ve known, with a new community of characters. A way to bring everyone back together would be great, however, but there’s still a lot of dust to settle.

I found the BM telegram stuff got a bit too “if you had an alternative opinion with criticism it was always wrong” and my god rich and his rules got on my tits. 

Don’t get me wrong I admire the work and effort of everyone involved but it was starting to grate on me with certain people and their always right opinions. 

Much prefer the kind of numbers in this group. I tried discord for about half hour and hated it so will just stick with here. Top work by

to get it set up 👍🏻💪🏻

A week or so in and we’ve not had any arguments or “you’re wrong” because we are (most of the time) adults who can respect other people’s opinions/views, even

‘s .

Happy Sunday everyone! I hope the Easter bunny all brought you a lovely Easter egg! I’m now off out for a fry up!

messages.text: This group is great and I really don’t want to set this group up as a rival to BM - they’re great and I’m happy to continue chip in financially to their growth - but you’ve got a bit of work on to grow this group. The BM group grows because the have a media brand. People watch YT, read the articles, maybe see the pin badges. This group has no external advertising to funnel people in. People will leave this group over time or just stop interacting, it’s just how it goes, so you probably need 1 or 2 new people month here just to retain parity with numbers.

messages.text:
On the subject of arguments I agree with what’s been said. We won’t all agree especially about games / performances and I’m all for an argument around that. But I’m hoping the general feeling of the group is people will have a different opinion and no one gets called out just for that alone. 

We could open the group up and try and grow it but doing it via social media would be a minefield for the type of lunatic you might land.. 

If anyone knows anyone else who they think would enjoy it feel free to recruit your own mates. We can provide a link for them to join. 

55 people should be able to generate enough conversation to keep us all going if we all participate. 

Or We could operate a one in one out policy 😉

Proof of life post. Happy to be a  lurker, rarely posted on BM.My irst game was 68 v QPR at Easter when we both went up.
Did PM Kev Ellis during the  championship season worried that McKenna s  set up was getting predictable for opposition managers, hoping he'd set my mind at rest- he didn't really.I assume it's part of the etiquette here to be rude to kev.
Therefore happy to be the grumpy lurker- although I'm not really that grumpy

Proof of life post. Happy to be a  lurker, rarely posted on BM.My irst game was 68 v QPR at Easter when we both went up.
Did PM Kev Ellis during the  championship season worried that McKenna s  set up was getting predictable for opposition managers, hoping he'd set my mind at rest- he didn't really.I assume it's part of the etiquette here to be rude to kev.

messages.text:
Incredible team to watch when they’re on it, but they really lack some quality wide players. So easy to set up against them if you sit back, cram the middle and stop them combining in those areas.

Although Swansea + Millwall have both pressed them high and at the moment are getting results from that, but I think that’s mainly the moments of self sabotage hurting them.

messages.text: I’m going to talk myself out of the quiet optimist corner and possibly into the bin - although I do still reckon we’ll go up - but I think we need to be happy with a draw against Norwich. They’ll be up for it, it’s their cup final, they’re on form and they have a chance to put a dent in our promotion bid. We’re playing the long game and aren’t exactly steamrolling teams, we feel a bit brittle. We’ll win the Pompey game in hand and drag it over the line, but playing Norwich is an unwelcome distraction. Let it be their cup final, not ours.

messages.text:
Even putting aside the sheer banter of signing him from Norwich, and whilst he doesn't have flashy numbers, must be an argument that Marcelino Nunez is the signing of the summer in the EFL, for just how much he makes us tick, and our record with and without him in the side.

Nunez starts in the 10 role - 44 points from 18 games - 2.44ppg
Nunez doesn't start in the 10 role - 27 points from 20 games - 1.35ppg

There’s probably a great Michael Palin style tv travelogue show in that:

Episode 1: Nine Cruzcampos between Manchester and Crewe
Episode 2: Smashing the Sainsbury’s 4 for 3 deal on craft beers between Exeter St David’s and Torquay
Episode 3: The Hogwarts Express with a crate of Stella
Episode 4: Struggling with gluten free beers on the way to Newcastle

The end of the program is either incomprehensible sleepy muttering or angrily berating passengers while the English countryside gently rolls past the window.

messages.text:
My wife and I used to measure the speed of a pub in DPH drinks per hour. In my early thirties our local was 2 DPH, which is a fast pub, but as we moved out eight years later it had become a 1.5 DPH (your pint takes 45 mins to drink). The metric started to unravel when we realised that fun atmospheric pubs, problematic flat roof pubs and boring non descript pubs all had a high DPH for different reasons, but we still enjoy pre judging then measuring the DPH of a pub.

Since we’ve had kids most pubs now have a 0 DPH.

text_entities.text:
My wife and I used to measure the speed of a pub in DPH drinks per hour. In my early thirties our local was 2 DPH, which is a fast pub, but as we moved out eight years later it had become a 1.5 DPH (your pint takes 45 mins to drink). The metric started to unravel when we realised that fun atmospheric pubs, problematic flat roof pubs and boring non descript pubs all had a high DPH for different reasons, but we still enjoy pre judging then measuring the DPH of a pub.

Since we’ve had kids most pubs now have a 0 DPH.

messages.text: Was trying to workout why I feel so meh about Saturday. Think it’s cause everything about this season suggests that we’ll lose the derby, but still go up in second. It’s a season of relative consistency but just lacking moments, the real highs. Even Saturday: our first from behind win, yet there was little drama, just two quick fire first-half goals. The double over Coventry was great… but would have been all the better were we properly challenging them. The 5-0 over Sheff Utd was when they were shocking. Got turned over at Boro. First game of the season was a big occasion and we didn’t show up. Whole thing has felt like a slog. And yet… if we buck the trend and win in style on Saturday it’ll be all the sweeter. I still just don’t quite have the faith in this group, sadly.

messages.text:
Speaking about McAteer.. Been tough for him. Taking him longer to settle in but really happy for him to score and get recognition.  Been training well. 

Re Johnson. Come in and been unbelievable in last few games. Good character to have around the group and been good all season in training etc.

text_entities.text:
Speaking about McAteer.. Been tough for him. Taking him longer to settle in but really happy for him to score and get recognition.  Been training well. 

Re Johnson. Come in and been unbelievable in last few games. Good character to have around the group and been good all season in training etc.

messages.text:
On the derby. Really looking forward to it. will be hostile and tough but looking forward to it. Looking to do the double over them. Speaking about the win earlier in season. Was probably a bigger achievement than he expected it to be. 

Asked if the group have spoken about what it would mean to win the double over them Yes they understand its been 28 years since it last happened.

messages.text: Asked about last 7 games and difference in playing teams with something to play for or those in middle of division. Mckenna said biggest challenge is the schedule more so than who you play.

text_entities.text: Asked about last 7 games and difference in playing teams with something to play for or those in middle of division. Mckenna said biggest challenge is the schedule more so than who you play.

messages.text:
Asked about the scheduling. In the past seasons have been extended to fit in games. Is there a case for that in the modern day.. 
KM I think that would be difficult for the integrity of the competition. For example if we both needed a point us and Southampton could just pass the ball around for 90 mins. 
He thinks there is something that could be done to help Championship teams who go deep in FA CUp and are still going for promotion like Southampton and Coventry last time round.

text_entities.text:
Asked about the scheduling. In the past seasons have been extended to fit in games. Is there a case for that in the modern day.. 
KM I think that would be difficult for the integrity of the competition. For example if we both needed a point us and Southampton could just pass the ball around for 90 mins. 
He thinks there is something that could be done to help Championship teams who go deep in FA CUp and are still going for promotion like Southampton and Coventry last time round.

messages.text:
Asked about Ashley Young.. Back training with the Group. 
Adds to the group every single day he is with us. 
Less cones for Martin to pick up..

text_entities.text:
Asked about Ashley Young.. Back training with the Group. 
Adds to the group every single day he is with us. 
Less cones for Martin to pick up..

messages.text:
Very few players will play all the minutes in the coming games. will need to utilize the strength of the group. 
Jaden had some niggles in the last bit of his return from injury. Got a kick v Millwall which caused him issues. 
He is working hard and is close to being back to where he wants to be. 

Asked about Jens. He is working hard, been a difficult spell for him. Not quite hit the heights he would have hoped to. 
Lots of reasons for that. His professionalism is really good and fully engaged. 
Championship is such a tough league to play in. Jens is one who has probably found that out. 
We have massive belief in him. Capable of playing for big clubs and in Europe and he will have that chance next season when I fuck him off..

messages.text: I don’t mind this hot take. Leif has the same problem that Trent AA has, in that they started playing football about 20 years after 442 fell out of fashion. He’d be an excellent left midfielder in a 442, able to whip in crosses from the left or a killer through ball, you’re not expected to finish much, and while there’s a need to put your foot in and win the ball back as a midfielder you’ve got a left back behind you better suited to defending.

text_entities.text: I don’t mind this hot take. Leif has the same problem that Trent AA has, in that they started playing football about 20 years after 442 fell out of fashion. He’d be an excellent left midfielder in a 442, able to whip in crosses from the left or a killer through ball, you’re not expected to finish much, and while there’s a need to put your foot in and win the ball back as a midfielder you’ve got a left back behind you better suited to defending.

messages.text: Obv Kieran won’t play Leif on the wing. In the promotion seasons KMK adapted by - according to the heat maps - telling Leif to GTF away from the defence and join the three attacking midfielders as a line of four behind the forward. When Leif bombed on the right back shuffled over and Morsy and Mass stayed put, giving a screen of two in front of a back three. I’m surprised we weren’t countered more on the wings, it lacks width. Going forwards we have a left winger who cuts in, Jaden/JC, giving Leif the space to overlap. Not sure what’s happened with the early season experiment to have Leif cut inside.

messages.text: This is the key thing for me too. I look at our position and what we need to do and should be relatively confident, but I can't trust the team to keep control of a game. I get that sometimes it's the wrong tactics, players missing or just bad luck that means games don't go your way. But a game can be going completely our way and under control and then ten minutes later it's lost.

messages.text:
KM’s comments on Kipre after Norwich (H)… Be amazed if he doesn’t start today.

"A brilliant performance. Long before the goal, when there was tension in the game on both teams, I thought he looked the most composed player on the pitch.
"It was everything we were hoping for, to be honest, because he has got that calm, laidback demeanour and in big games sometimes that can be really useful.
"Gave a real composure to the team, dealt with the threat really, really well, was calm on the ball and even better he stepped up and smashed a goal in.”

text_entities.text:
KM’s comments on Kipre after Norwich (H)… Be amazed if he doesn’t start today.

"A brilliant performance. Long before the goal, when there was tension in the game on both teams, I thought he looked the most composed player on the pitch.
"It was everything we were hoping for, to be honest, because he has got that calm, laidback demeanour and in big games sometimes that can be really useful.
"Gave a real composure to the team, dealt with the threat really, really well, was calm on the ball and even better he stepped up and smashed a goal in.”

messages.text: I’ve got my home shirt on today. My birthday is July so I usually get it each summer, my sister in law got it for me last year. As it was my 40th she stuck my name and 40 on the back - I smiled through it when she turned it around - so now whenever I’m wearing this season’s shirt I feel I’m on a one man stag do.

messages.text:
That was a massive theme in the BM chat earlier this season with many insisting the rotation was killing us until we steadily got results and it faded away as ‘thing’. 

Today is less about the rotation,  more the idea we’ve somehow not lined up in a ‘toe to toe’ take em on fashion?

Today is less about the rotation,  more the idea we’ve somehow not lined up in a ‘toe to toe’ take em on fashion?

messages.text: The three behind Hirst haven’t excelled (penalty aside). messages.text:
Have I got this right?…


Today we beat Norwich ✔️ ✔️ ✔️ 

So did the league double over them for the first time since 1992/93 ✔️ 

Which effectively ended their play off chances ✔️ 

Plus Millwall drew yesterday ✔️ 

And Middlesbrough lost today ✔️ 

Which means we’re two points clear in the automatic promotion places ✔️ 

With two games in hand on our rivals ✔️

Meaning we just need ten points from our final six games to go up ✔️

messages.text: Not meaning to bring the tone down, but I remember walking down to Carrow Road as an away fan many years ago and hearing that Justin Fashanu song. I was only a teenager at the time, so I didn’t fully understand it, but I felt disgusted when I realised what the words were and what the song was about. I really hope there are no fans still singing that, and I would definitely speak up if I heard it being sung now!

text_entities.text: Not meaning to bring the tone down, but I remember walking down to Carrow Road as an away fan many years ago and hearing that Justin Fashanu song. I was only a teenager at the time, so I didn’t fully understand it, but I felt disgusted when I realised what the words were and what the song was about. I really hope there are no fans still singing that, and I would definitely speak up if I heard it being sung now!

messages.text: Same happened to me at the 4-1 game when Holt scored a hat trick. Couldn’t believe what I was hearing. Every fanbase seems to have a minority of absolute pondlife unfortunately.

messages.text:
There was a lot of head shaking and tutting when Fabrizio Romano took the Saudi money and started tweeting how great Saudi is as a place to visit and do business. That led to a few articles analysing him and his methods and journalists denouncing him as no longer one of their own.

His business model being to accept money and send tweets/mentions on behalf of players agitating for a move, clubs wanting it look like they’re a buying club to their fans, and selling clubs wanting to drum up interest and create an auction.

…Is Alan Nixon still a journalist or is he now an EFL Romano?

If the latter, I guess there’s a difference between reading the headline and understanding why he’s written that headline. There’s probably something to learn from both but it might not be the same thing.

Are we in for Ruthsworth, are Brighton scaring other suitors to act now or is Ruthsworth’s agent linking him to the calibre of club he’d like to eventually move to?

messages.text:
Totally agree. I was right Against the divide and the coppers were bang on there. Some of our fans were less so. Some hilarious banter as well. 
When we arrived at the ground it was dangerously rammed. Really oppressive and tight. Pleased to get in the stand.  

There was a really big guy near us in the home end who got some serious shit. 

Train station was fine both times too. The train to Cambridge was funny. Lots of songs. Some tension. Some idiots. But most people were decent - both sides.

text_entities.text:
Totally agree. I was right Against the divide and the coppers were bang on there. Some of our fans were less so. Some hilarious banter as well. 
When we arrived at the ground it was dangerously rammed. Really oppressive and tight. Pleased to get in the stand.  

There was a really big guy near us in the home end who got some serious shit. 

Train station was fine both times too. The train to Cambridge was funny. Lots of songs. Some tension. Some idiots. But most people were decent - both sides.

messages.text: I’m going to get chucked out of the group for this, but we have been ridiculously lucky. Our goal against Brum shouldn’t have stood and Nunez should have gone. The pen was soft but we still scored twice against Nodge so would have won 1-0 without it, but my fear is we’re winning without playing well. The cliche is that’s the mark of a champion (or a promotion winner), but we’ve still got six hard games to play and we’re not on top form.

messages.text:
I also think we’re ‘the bad guys’ now. We’ve gone from being the most average Championship of Championship clubs - 14 seasons in a row - to the pity of the Many Pauls era, to the adulation of double promotion. Now we’re favourites with evil parachute payments our rich American private equity owners are being mentioned more and expectations are raised and harder to meet. “You’ve spent how much? You should be walking the league.”

This all predates Farage but the last couple of weeks has probably cemented our image as whatever the opposite of a cuddly family club is.

messages.text: Seeing Carrow on Saturday reminded me of when we went on a night out in Norwich c 2000. We had been clubbing and we decided to break into Carrow Road. Was when the Riverside area was being built and it was incredibly easy to jump over the fence into the stadium. 7/8 of us had a kick around in the main pitch, sat in the stand and in the dug out, etc. Would be completely impossible to do these days.

text_entities.text: Seeing Carrow on Saturday reminded me of when we went on a night out in Norwich c 2000. We had been clubbing and we decided to break into Carrow Road. messages.text: Lo, the chief matter is the boldness Kmac holds that we shall break the PL this day, if we dare to rise. For if he stands fast in his own strength and in the house of the club, our roof shall be higher than the dwellings of Bournemouth, though this promise is for days to come. Yet, their band of men is now mightier than our own, and in the short time, they offer him a surer path to the sixth or eighth place, and a swifter spring to the great lords, so it seems to me.

text_entities.text: Lo, the chief matter is the boldness Kmac holds that we shall break the PL this day, if we dare to rise. For if he stands fast in his own strength and in the house of the club, our roof shall be higher than the dwellings of Bournemouth, though this promise is for days to come. Yet, their band of men is now mightier than our own, and in the short time, they offer him a surer path to the sixth or eighth place, and a swifter spring to the great lords, so it seems to me.

messages.text:
There's two positions where most weeks there's a bit of debate about who should play which are becoming pretty clear for me for the run in.

 Kipré has been really good for most of the season but his form has fallen off a cliff the last month or so, Greaves' shirt now next to O'Shea.

And Dan Neil just hasn't been what we are after at all, lacks physicality, doesn't offer enough for the ball, and loses possession so often. Taylor far from perfect in that role but offers much more of what we need in there I think, especially whilst Matusiwa isn't 100%.

messages.text: I tend to avoid this and other groups after a bad result, I can’t be bothered with the generational head loss. The 209 messages after full time were 50% looking for someone, something, anything to blame to rationalise it and make it all make sense, and the other 50% stood behind their squad car with a megaphone out, trying to talk them into releasing sanity from their headlock and slowly put the vegetable knife on the floor.

messages.text:
The guys I sit with in the Pioneer Upper have all said that you just can't trust this team. 

They go from sublime performances against Cov or Norwich and then phone one in the next game.

We've not gone on any long winning streak this season in what is arguably a weaker league. 

I am not convinced we will beat Boro and less so about getting out of St Mary's with a point. 

It's reminiscent of Magics team , where they suddenly realised if they got promoted they'd all be booted out. 

You wonder if this has dawned on some of them because they aren't pulling up trees in the championship

messages.text:
I was sat on a barstool in the Dedham Sun (before it got really posh) in the summer of 2000

At the bar was a very drunk centre half who played for Ipswich, previously of Wolves, with thunderous left foot. 
We had a chat about promotion, the PO final and the prospect of the Prem

He had the right hump as he thought that he'd be let go/sold as he wouldn't be deemed good enough to make the step up. Didn't want to move club, move house, etc etc

That didn't work out that way, but was interesting to actually hear it is a thing

text_entities.text:
I was sat on a barstool in the Dedham Sun (before it got really posh) in the summer of 2000

At the bar was a very drunk centre half who played for Ipswich, previously of Wolves, with thunderous left foot. 
We had a chat about promotion, the PO final and the prospect of the Prem

He had the right hump as he thought that he'd be let go/sold as he wouldn't be deemed good enough to make the step up. Didn't want to move club, move house, etc etc

That didn't work out that way, but was interesting to actually hear it is a thing

messages.text: Worst case we need 10 points from 5 games. But I honestly cannot see the other 3 winning all their games, and we have to play 2 of them. Completely in our own hands, and we do better against the better teams. Assume they each draw one game, that means we need 8 points from 5 games, 2 wins, 2 draws. We could probably (but shouldn’t) go into Middlesbrough and Saints for a draw. That would actually be the percentage play.

messages.text:
Happened to my local team in Spain last year and it was an absolute disaster. 

They were in a strong position to win the playoffs and in decent form, then the president made a batshit decision to get rid of the very well liked coach 2 games before the end of the season. I didn’t renew my season ticket as a result for the first time in 10 years.

Nastic went on to lose the playoff final after the new coach wanted to stamp his authority on the team and changed the tactics. He was then sacked a few months into this season and the club’s now had 3 permanent managers this season alone. And there’s a chance of going down too.

The grass most certainly isn’t always greener!

It’s a tough league this year.

messages.text:
To be fair I don’t think many clubs recruited that well last summer in comparison. Especially those that went down. The market is so inflated right now.

I just hope we don’t go the same route again in the summer ,and spend the vast majority of our transfer fund on the UK market like we did last time we got promoted.  Signing the likes of Matusiwa is proof we can find some bargains abroad. 

Walle-Egeli hasn’t done anywhere near as well as hoped but he’s still very young and has a high ceiling. There are many examples of young players that have been signed for big money that take time to fully develop and don’t make an immediate impact. Hopefully after a full and, fingers crossed, injury free pre season he’ll start to put in improved performances.

messages.text: Part of me worries that Nunez is deep undercover. He’s going to clear off back to Norwich in the summer, forcing a move for a fiver and flicking Vs to everyone, waving their corner flags and showing off everything he’s nicked from the club shop. I just don’t understand how he flipped so drastically from being adored by Norwich to hating them that much over one summer. Show us where they touched you, Marcelino. Yes, he’s clearly not popular for crossing the divide, but most players just keep their heads down and it was their chief exec got it in the neck for the sale and the low fee more than Nunez. Unless he’s just a dog that chases the loudest car.

messages.text:
Think the issue he has and we all love a reason to slag a player off is that when he signed he was on big money compared to those players who got us up. I heard £40k.
When we released salary figures for last season and you divided that by number of players in squad you came to about that number on average. I’m assuming they all got a fairly hefty decrease with relegation which is why DoS signed a new contract to probably take his out . 

So yes he’s on big money but relatively speaking for the level I doubt it would stop someone if they wanted him
